member.name: Greg Smith
member.synopsis: Greg Smith is the Conservative MP for Mid Buckinghamshire , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019. He currently undertakes the roles of Shadow Parliamentary Under Secretary (Energy Security and Net Zero), and Shadow Parliamentary Under Secretary (Transport).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Lobbying: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether his Department has issued guidance to departments and arm's length bodies on the use of public funds for public affairs, external affairs and lobbying activities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cabinet Office has not issued specific additional guidance on the use of public funds for public affairs and lobbying activities. Section 4.1 of the Code of Conduct for Board Members of Public Bodies states that it is an improper use of public funds f...

writtenQuestions.heading: Motor Vehicles: China
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of the European Union’s consideration of further tariffs on vehicles manufactured in China, including plug-in hybrid vehicle on the UK au
writtenQuestions.answerText: We continue to engage with the European Commission on industrial policy and will assess the potential impact of any policy measures if they are formally announced. Where there is sufficient evidence of harm caused by unfair trade practices, industries may...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what estimate she has made of the annual cost to the Exchequer of extending eligibility for government-backed student finance to standalone commercial pilot training courses leading to Civil Aviation Authority
writtenQuestions.answerText: Student finance is designed to support higher education study leading to an academic award. Eligible students can apply for student finance for the academic elements of designated pilot training degree programmes.Courses designated for student finance are...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Alternative Fuels
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the answer of 26 June 2026 to Question 11220 on Aviation: Alternative Fuels, whether her Department has made an assessment of the potential impact of Sustainable Aviation Fuel Mandate compliance cos
writtenQuestions.answerText: As previously stated, an assessment of the costs of SAF policies and the impact on air fares was conducted as part of the published SAF Mandate cost-benefit analysis. The SAF Mandate is designed to protect against excessive costs being passed on to passen...

writtenQuestions.heading: Airports: Parking
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 29 June 2026 to Question 11465 on Airports: Fees and Charges, if her Department will have discussions with airport operators on the implementation of parking fees and charges.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department regularly engages with airport operators on surface access matters and will continue to discuss issues relating to airport access, including parking arrangements, as part of that engagement.

writtenQuestions.heading: Large Goods Vehicles: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 29 June 2026 to Question 12905 on Large Goods Vehicles: Electric Vehicles, whether her Department has made an assessment of the potential cost savings for businesses operating battery-
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer of 29 June 2026 to Question 12905.

writtenQuestions.heading: Air Traffic Control: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what estimate her Department has made of the annual number of air traffic controllers expected to leave the profession over the next five years; and how many newly qualified air traffic controllers will be requ
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to Question UIN 14936 answered on 6 July.

writtenQuestions.answerText: The table below shows the number of individuals that had initial UK Part-66 aircraft maintenance licences issued over the past five years. It does not show renewals or changes to existing licences. A licence once issued is valid for 5 years. Nationality Y...

writtenQuestions.heading: Network Rail: Biodiversity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 24 June 2026 to Question 11452 on Network Rail: Biodiversity, what guidance Network Rail issues on when a habitat management plan is required; and how many such plans are currently in
writtenQuestions.answerText: Network Rail has a company standard entitled ‘Biodiversity’ published in March 2023. Within this standard is a module entitled ‘Habitat Management Plan’ and its scope reads: ‘This module contains the key principles for the management and enhancement of bi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Vehicle Certification Agency: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 25 March 2026 to Question 122545 on the Vehicle Certification Agency, when she expects the evaluation of the consultation on proposed Vehicle Certification Agency fee increases to be c
writtenQuestions.answerText: The evaluation of consultation responses about the fee increases proposed by the Vehicle Certification Agency (VCA) is now complete. The response to that consultation is available on the VCA website.

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Transport: Public Relations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department has issued any directions to its arm's length bodies on limiting expenditure on public affairs, government relations or lobbying activity since 4 July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Accounting Officers of DfT public bodies are bound by Managing Public Money and Cabinet Office guidance. The Department reminds individual bodies, as necessary, of these rules and the expectations set out in section 4.1 of the Code of Conduct for Board Me...

writtenQuestions.heading: Vans: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assumptions her Department has made about the average daily mileage of light commercial vans when assessing the charging infrastructure required to support the transition to zero emission vans.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Modelled estimates of the charging infrastructure required to support the transition to zero emission vans were included in the 2022 Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Strategy. An annual vans mileage assumption range of 11,500 to 14,000 was included within ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Large Goods Vehicles: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 22 June 2026 to Question 8651 on Large Goods Vehicles: Electric Vehicles, what estimate her Department has made of the proportion of heavy goods vehicle journeys for which charging can
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department expect that most electric Heavy Goods Vehicles will be recharged overnight. For those which are not, we expect they will be recharged during mandatory driver rest breaks or during periods when they are stopping for other reasons. An analysi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 22 June 2026 to Question 7511, whether the review of the Zero Emission Vehicle Mandate will have published terms of reference; and if so, on what date she expects those terms of refere
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government does not intend to publish terms of reference for the Zero Emission Vehicle Mandate Review. The terms of the review are for the UK Government and the devolved governments to determine, with a public consultation to follow in due course.

writtenQuestions.heading: South West Main Line: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many delay minutes per 100 train miles were recorded on South Western Railway in the (a) final year before transfer into public ownership and (b) latest year for which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: South Western Railway's (SWR) on self delay minutes per 1000 train miles at the end of May 2025 was 10.59. The same metric at the end of May 2026 was 11.82. SWR inherited delays to the introduction of the new Arterio fleet and a shortage of trained driver...

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Secutiry Benefits: Veterans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, how many veterans with (a) amputations and (b) other permanent injuries were reassessed for disability-related benefits in each of the last three years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information could only be provided at disproportionate cost.

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans: Disadvantaged
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what progress he has made on ensuring that no veteran is disadvantaged as a result of military service.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Armed Forces Covenant is a promise by the nation to support our Armed Forces community. The promise is to ensure that those who serve and have served, along with their families and the bereaved, are treated fairly. In June 2025, the Prime Minister unv...

writtenQuestions.heading: Office for Veterans' Affairs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to improve the coordinating role of the Office for Veterans' Affairs across government.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In November 2025, the Government published its new 10-year Veterans Strategy that sets a new narrative around the remarkable men and women who have served in our Armed Forces. This strategy challenges the whole of society to think differently about those ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans: Disability Aids
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of long term funding arrangements for the Veterans Mobility Fund and Veterans Hearing Loss Fund on veterans.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Veterans’ Mobility Fund provides vital support to veterans seeking to enhance their mobility, independence, and overall quality of life. The fund is designed to support veterans with physical disabilities resulting from illness or injury sustained dur...

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans: Disability Aids
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what discussions his Department has had regarding the future of the Veterans Mobility Fund beyond March 2027.
